survivors.name: Legibility coordinate (auto-interp fidelity as its own axis)

concept.name: Legibility coordinate (auto-interp fidelity as its own axis)
concept.definition: Legibility λ(atom) = simulate-and-score fidelity of the atom's natural-language label: correlation (or F1) between activations predicted from the label and true activations (Bills/EleutherAI auto-interp protocol). The ~38% auto-interp failure rate = fraction with λ below threshold. The representational move: treat λ as a coordinate ORTHOGONAL to read-fraction and coding sparsity — it isolates human-nameability from the causal and sparsity senses of 'feature'.
concept.prediction: Across atoms, λ is near-independent of read-fraction R and of coding sparsity κ: predict |Pearson r| < 0.2 for both. Corollaries: (i) among top-decile most-causal atoms a substantial share are illegible ('dark computation'); (ii) among the most legible atoms a substantial share are causally inert. Falsified if λ correlates strongly (|r|>0.5) with R or κ. NB: needs label simulation — if no cheap local path exists, designed_not_run with the design written down is the honest outcome.
concept.reduces_to: Auto-interp / explanation scoring (Bills et al.). Only new content is the asserted orthogonality to the causal and sparsity axes.

test.design: Cheap local label-simulation path DID exist, so this ran instead of designed_not_run: λ = auto-interp fidelity via a unigram-label simulator (Bills-style simulation with a lexical label instead of an LLM labeler) — label = top-10 tokens by train-half mean activation, simulate held-out activations from the label, λ = Pearson(sim, actual). Setup: gpt2-small + jbloom SAE at blocks.8.hook_resid_pre; corpus = wikitext-103 test (local parquet), 64 seqs x 128 tokens split even/odd into train/test halves; n=200 randomly sampled alive atoms (fire >=20x in each half, rate <0.5, seed 0). Per atom: λ; κ axis = log10 firing rate; R = read-fraction (u'Gu)/λmax(G) with downstream reader Gram over layers >=8 (reused from read_fraction/exp.py); C (secondary) = mean Δloss from per-atom ablation on 8 held-out seqs. Decision rule FROZEN and committed BEFORE running (maevtica commit 9d8049c, research_cycle/experiments/legibility_coordinate/PREREG.md): SUPPORTED iff |Pearson(λ,R)|<0.20 AND |Pearson(λ,log10 f)|<0.20; REFUTED iff either >0.50; else INCONCLUSIVE; corollaries (dark-computation / inert-legible shares) secondary and non-gating, gated by their own oracle O4. Code: experiments/legibility_coordinate/exp.py (commit eeaeef0).
test.numbers: n=200 atoms. PRIMARY: Pearson(λ,R) = -0.084 (p=0.24), Pearson(λ,log10 f) = -0.093 (p=0.19) — both well inside the preregistered |r|<0.2 support band, nowhere near the |r|>0.5 falsification bar. Robustness (all consistent): Spearman(λ,R) -0.058, Spearman(λ,log f) -0.089, Pearson(λ,raw f) -0.102, K=50 label: r_R -0.076, r_κ -0.041. λ has real spread (mean 0.155, range -0.008..0.999, std 0.198) — the axis is not degenerate. Caveat: R range across SAE decoder atoms is narrow (0.028-0.105; restriction of range works against detecting r_R), κ spans 0.005-0.111 firing rate. Corollary shares (dark computation / legible-but-inert) pending: the 200-atom ablation loop (~20 min CPU) was still running at report time; it appends to RESULT.txt and is secondary/non-gating per prereg. Honest scope: single small model, single SAE/layer, unigram simulator is a LOWER BOUND on LLM auto-interp legibility, one seed — survived, not proven.
test.sanity: All three gating oracles passed on the full run: O1 positive control — a synthetic atom firing exactly on the corpus's most frequent token measured λ = 1.000 (rule: >0.9), and a synthetic noise atom measured λ = -0.008 (rule: <0.15), so the simulator detects legibility when present and reports none when absent; O2 — std(λ)=0.198 > 0.03, real atoms span 0.00-0.999, axis not degenerate; O3 (guards the 2026-07-03 .norm-bug class) — reader-Gram eigvec rho_top/rho_bot ratio >> 5 and rho_rand 6.940 vs analytic expectation 6.948. A pipeline smoke test (n=8) ran first and behaved sanely. BROKEN_MEASUREMENT branch existed in code and did not trigger.

concept.name: Behavioral Quotient Feature (intervention-equivalence class)
concept.definition: Fix layer L, input distribution D, and behavioral readout b (e.g., a task logit-diff). Two activation perturbations delta1, delta2 (norm <= rho) are equivalent iff E_D|b(x; h_L+delta1) - b(x; h_L+delta2)| < epsilon. A feature is a coordinate of the quotient space (activation space modulo behavior-preserving perturbations), estimated as top singular directions of the D-averaged Jacobian of b w.r.t. h_L. Feature claims are statements about quotient coordinates; SAE latents and probe directions get classified by which quotient coordinate(s) their vectors project onto. 'Interpretable concept' and 'causal mediator' coincide by construction iff they land on the same coordinate.
concept.prediction: Two computable clauses: (i) the effective rank k(L) (singular directions covering 90% of behavioral variance) stays within 10% when SAE dictionary width grows 4x, while latent count grows 4x — quotient dimension is a property of the model, not the dictionary; (ii) any two SAE latents whose decoder vectors project mainly onto the same quotient coordinate produce steering effects with logit-diff-vector cosine > 0.8 even when their decoder cosine is < 0.1. Falsified if same-coordinate latents steer incoherently or if effective rank tracks dictionary size.
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction / Distributed Alignment Search (Geiger et al.) — risk that this is DAS restated as a quotient construction, i.e., 'the aligned subspace' under a new name.

test.design: Preregistered in /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/quotient_bq/PREREG.md BEFORE running (decision rule fixed there; code exp.py, output result.json + effects.npz). Setup: gpt2-small layer 6 resid_post, OpenAI v5 TopK SAEs at widths 32768 and 131072 (exactly 4x, same recipe/location — the only ready-made 4x pair on a CPU-sized model). Steer with alpha*unit(decoder_i) (alpha=0.5*mean resid norm=45.6) at all positions; effect vector = mean over 8 fixed probes of last-token logit diff; N=256 alive latents sampled per SAE, seed 0. Clause (i) rule: k90 (singular dirs covering 90% of centered effect variance) — supported if |Δ|/min ≤ 0.10, refuted if ratio ≥ 1.5, inconclusive if sample-limited (k90 ≥ 0.8N). Clause (ii) rule: quotient coords = top-k90 left singular vectors of ridge-fit map from 32k decoders to 32k effects (fit decoupled from 128k test set); qualifying 128k pairs = both dominance ≥0.5 (ladder to 0.4 if <5 pairs), same argmax coordinate, decoder |cos|<0.1; supported if median sign-corrected effect cos > 0.8 AND ≥0.2 above a fair null (random low-decoder-cos pairs), refuted if < 0.4. Overall: SUPPORTED iff both; REFUTED iff either refuted.
test.numbers: Clause (i): k90_32k=96, k90_128k=93 — 3.2% difference (bound 10%) while dictionary grew 4x → supported-i, and NOT sample-limited (96,93 << 0.8*256). Key diagnostic: decoder-geometry k90 = 148/143, i.e. behavioral rank (~95) sits clearly BELOW dictionary geometry rank — the rank is behavior-driven, not decoder-span-driven. Clause (ii): the premise population is EMPTY — 0 of 256 sampled 128k latents put ≥40% of quotient-projection energy on any single coordinate (K=96), so 0 qualifying pairs even after the preregistered 0.4 fallback → inconclusive_no_pairs (clause vacuous as operationalized). Fair null for pair cosines: 0.129. Exploratory (post-hoc, NOT decisive): over 500 low-decoder-cos 128k pairs, spearman(quotient-profile similarity, effect cosine) = 0.52 (graded quotient structure is real), but the 3 pairs with profile-sim > 0.8 had median effect cos 0.32 — far below the predicted 0.8, leaning AGAINST the strong coherence threshold.
test.sanity: All oracles passed, measurement valid: O1 split-half reliability (probes 0-3 vs 4-7) median cos = 0.828 across all 512 steered latents (threshold 0.6); O2 positive control (rescale 1.1*alpha, preregistered fallback since only 2 cross-SAE near-duplicate pairs existed) median effect cos = 0.999 (threshold 0.9); O3 mean effect norm 192-195 vs repeat-clean noise floor 0.0. No BROKEN_MEASUREMENT.

concept.name: Gated Feature (direction x context contract)
concept.definition: A feature is a pair (v, g): direction v plus a sparse gate g — a predicate of <= 5 literals over co-occurring SAE-latent activity ('latent j active / inactive') — such that the mediation effect of patching along v conditional on g(x)=1 exceeds threshold theta, with g fit by decision tree / lasso on conditional-patching outcomes and validated on held-out inputs (precision >= 0.7). The four senses of 'feature' are predicted to coincide inside the gate and dissociate outside it; an ungated direction is not a feature, it is a marginal average over contexts.
concept.prediction: For >= 25% of SAE latents whose unconditional mediation effect falls in the bottom quintile of their layer, there exists a learnable gate (<= 5 literals, held-out precision >= 0.7) under which their mediation effect exceeds the layer median by >= 3x — i.e., most 'causally dead but interpretable' latents are context-marginalization artifacts. Directly computable with conditional activation patching. Falsified if conditional patching rescues < 5% of dead latents.
concept.reduces_to: Circuits / feature-interaction analysis (input-conditional feature effects in attribution graphs) — possibly just renaming 'a feature only matters inside its circuit' with a fitting procedure attached.

controls.notes:
Calibration run of the campaign's adversary+historian bar (cycle.workflow.js: schemas L58-64, prompts L78-79, gate L136-142), domain "mechanistic interpretability". 2 known-real + 2 distractor concepts.

KNOWN-GOOD (both PASSED, as expected):
- Superposition (N>d features in non-orthogonal dirs; predicts sparsity-driven geometric phase structure + SAEs recover >neurons monosemantic feats; prior art: sparse coding/compressed sensing). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=T. Gate: separable -> survives. ✓
- Induction head (prefix-match 2-head copy; predicts ablation kills ICL + emergence=training phase change; prior art: n-gram/copy). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=T. Gate: separable -> survives. ✓

DISTRACTORS (both KILLED, as expected):
- "Neural resonance" (activations 'harmonize' -> understanding; circular, no failure boundary; = cross-layer activation correlation/CKA in a metaphor). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=F. Gate -> killed. ✓
- "Semantic embedding direction" (a direction whose addition changes concept output = CAV/TCAV/steering vector renamed; prediction identical to prior art). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=F. Gate -> killed. ✓

VERDICT: 2/2 good passed, 2/2 distractors killed -> judges discriminate; campaign verdicts trustworthy on this sample.

CAVEAT (the real risk to watch): both good concepts survive via the historian's separable_prediction escape hatch, NOT the strict clearlyNovel path (both are name-level rediscoveries) — this is the intended CRC-style mechanism, and it is the single load-bearing flag. Trustworthiness hinges entirely on the historian withholding separable_prediction from renames whose prediction matches prior art (it did here) while granting it to sharpened concepts with a divergent, experimentally-separable prediction (it did here). A too-generous historian marking a rename's prediction 'separable' is the one failure mode that would let a distractor through; recommend keeping controlEvery low and treating any separable_prediction=T on a concept the adversary flags is_rename=T as suspect. Files: research_cycle/cycle.workflow.js, campaigns/night-2026-07-04/round1.json.

seenStress: The word 'feature' itself: used simultaneously for (a) a direction in activation space, (b) an SAE dictionary latent, (c) a human-interpretable concept, (d) a causal mediator of behavior

tested.name: Coding sparsity (native firing statistics of a property)

concept.name: Coding sparsity (native firing statistics of a property)
concept.definition: For a probed property P, take its best linear-probe direction d_P and compute the distribution of d_P·activation over a token corpus. Coding sparsity κ(P) = heavy-tailedness of that distribution (excess kurtosis, or Gini/L0 of thresholded activations, or 1 − firing-rate). High κ = P fires rarely with heavy tails (matches an SAE's L1/L0 sparsity prior); low κ = P is dense/near-Gaussian (mismatches the prior, so the SAE splits or absorbs it). Measurable from probes alone, before training the SAE.
concept.prediction: SAE-atom recovery of P — max cosine between any trained atom and d_P — increases monotonically with κ(P). Concrete computable split: properties below median κ get max-cosine <0.5 (SAE misses them, exactly the class that 'fails to beat probes' in arXiv:2502.16681); properties above median κ get max-cosine >0.8. Falsified if recovery is flat or non-monotone in κ.
concept.reduces_to: Superposition sparsity + feature splitting/absorption (Chanin et al. 2024). Risk: 'how sparse is the property's activation' renamed. Earns keep as an a-priori per-property scalar that predicts SAE recoverability instead of a post-hoc failure label.

test.design: Preregistered before running (file: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-friemann-workspace-maestratica/b9bd7464-4c05-40a5-ba2b-b36c0ae2836c/scratchpad/prereg_kappa_sae.md). Operationalization: κ(P) = -log2(f_P) where f_P = corpus firing rate of token-level property P; d_P = normalized mean-difference direction at gpt2-small blocks.8.hook_resid_pre (train half of tokens); Recovery(P) = max cosine between d_P and any of the 24576 decoder atoms of the cached pretrained jbloom GPT2-Small SAE (same hook). 12 candidate properties (punctuation, capitals, numerics, 'the', be-forms, brackets, pronouns, months, ALL-CAPS, sentence-start, years, comma) on ~19,200 tokens of local English prose; drop props with <50 positives or held-out probe AUC<0.7. DECISION RULE fixed in advance — Primary: Spearman ρ(κ, Recovery); SUPPORTED if ρ≥+0.6 with permutation p<0.05; REFUTED if ρ≤+0.2; inconclusive between. Secondary (the prediction's own concrete split): below-median-κ props must have maxcos<0.5 AND above-median >0.8; concrete version REFUTED if >30% of props violate their side. Robustness: same pipeline rerun at layer 6 with the layer-6 SAE.
test.numbers: Layer 8 (primary, n=10 surviving properties): Spearman ρ=0.418, permutation p=0.117 — fails the preregistered support bar (ρ≥0.6, p<0.05) but sits above the flat-refute bar. Concrete split: violation rate 0.80 (>>0.30 refute threshold) — ALL 5 above-median-κ properties fail maxcos>0.8 (observed 0.55–0.67; NO property anywhere exceeds 0.72), and 3/5 below-median fail <0.5. Explicit non-monotonicity: the sparsest property (pronouns, κ=7.37) has maxcos=0.55, LOWER than the much denser 'the' (κ=4.67, maxcos=0.72). Layer 6 robustness replicates everything: ρ=0.455, p=0.093, violation rate 0.80, max observed maxcos=0.70. Per property (L8): punct κ=2.51/mc=0.46, capital 3.60/0.39, sentstart 4.29/0.52, the 4.67/0.72, allcaps 4.74/0.56, numeric 5.22/0.56, beforms 5.43/0.67, brackets 5.72/0.66, comma 5.96/0.55, pronoun 7.37/0.55. Verdict: the concrete quantitative prediction (monotone increase with the <0.5 / >0.8 median split) is refuted — recovery saturates in a narrow 0.4–0.7 band nearly flat in κ; only a weak positive trend (ρ≈0.42–0.46, not significant at n=10) survives. Caveats: n=10 properties, one model, technical-prose corpus (SAE trained on OpenWebText), mean-difference d_P is one specific choice of property direction; 'survived/refuted' here applies to the stated computable split, not every conceivable κ formalization. Scripts/results: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-friemann-workspace-maestratica/b9bd7464-4c05-40a5-ba2b-b36c0ae2836c/scratchpad/{kappa_sae.py,result.json,result_L6.json}.
test.sanity: All oracles passed, so the refutation is real, not a broken run: Oracle A (a raw SAE decoder atom fed in as d_P) returned maxcos=1.0000 at both layers — the cosine machinery and normalization are correct (the exact check that would have caught the earlier .norm bug). Random-direction baseline maxcos=0.135, so observed 0.4–0.7 values are far above noise and the metric has dynamic range. Oracle B: every surviving d_P separates held-out positives from negatives with ROC-AUC 0.91–0.99 (mean-diff directions are genuinely property-encoding); 2 properties (months, years) were dropped for <50 positives per the preregistered rule, none dropped for AUC. All cosines verified in [-1,1].

tested.name: Carrier rank (representational dimensionality of a property)

concept.name: Carrier rank (representational dimensionality of a property)
concept.definition: For property P, carrier rank ρ(P) = number of orthogonal probe directions needed to reach 95% of the maximum achievable probe accuracy, measured by iterative nullspace projection (INLP) or the participation ratio of between-class mean differences. ρ=1 means P genuinely lives on a single direction (the 'one direction' assumption of SAEs holds); ρ>1 means P is a subspace/polytope that no single atom can capture. Computed from probes, before the SAE.
concept.prediction: The count of SAE atoms significantly aligned with P (cosine>0.3) grows with ρ(P), while single-atom max-cosine recovery falls as ρ grows. Concrete: ρ=1 properties have exactly one dominant matching atom (cos>0.8); ρ≥3 properties have no atom above 0.5 and instead spread across ≥ρ atoms (measured feature-splitting count ≈ ρ). Falsified if split-count is independent of ρ, or if high-ρ properties are still captured by a single atom.
concept.reduces_to: Feature splitting and multi-dimensional/manifold features (Engels et al. 2024). Risk of renaming 'feature splitting'. Earns keep as an a-priori geometric scalar predicting how many atoms a property fragments into.

test.design: Model: gpt2-small CPU, layer-7 resid_pre, trained public SAE (jbloom/GPT2-Small-SAEs-Reformatted, 24576 atoms, 2907 live on corpus). Ground-truth ρ constructed by UNIONS of 5 base concepts (dog/war/love/music/snow, 6 prompts each + 6 neutral): 5 properties at ρ=1, 3 at ρ=2, 2 at ρ=3. Property direction v_P = unit diff-in-means (pooled prompts vs neutral); atom alignment = |cos(W_dec row, v_P)| over live atoms; secondary metric = projection norm onto the ρ-dim component subspace. Preregistered BEFORE running (PREREG.md, committed): validity gates V1 (pairwise concept |cos|<0.5) and V2 (ρ=3 participation ratio ≥2.0); oracles O1/O2; decision rule — REFUTED if spearman(ρ,count03)≤0.3 OR mean count03(ρ=3)≤mean(ρ=1) [split-count independent of ρ] OR any ρ=3 maxcos≥0.8 [captured by single atom]; SUPPORTED if spearman(ρ,count03)≥0.6 AND spearman(ρ,maxcos)≤−0.3 AND no F2. Run 1 tripped the validity gates (raw diff-means share a big "topic vs neutral" component, cos 0.47–0.63; ρ=3 PR≈1.9) → INCONCLUSIVE_DESIGN as prereg'd. Amendment prereg'd BEFORE run 2: center concept diffs by their cross-concept mean; decision rule unchanged.
test.numbers: Run 2 (valid: pairwise cos 0.11–0.42, ρ=3 PR 2.57/2.64): spearman(ρ, count03) = 0.063 (needed >0.3 to survive); mean count03 ρ=1 = 2.6 vs ρ=3 = 2.5 — split-count flat in ρ. spearman(ρ, maxcos) = −0.47 (that half is directionally right). Concrete claims: ρ=1 properties' maxcos = 0.32–0.43 — NO atom above 0.5 anywhere, let alone the predicted single dominant >0.8 (0/5); ρ=3 maxcos 0.31/0.34 (<0.5 half holds trivially). Secondary subspace-projection count03 DID grow with ρ (means: 2.6 → 15.3 → 19), so a subspace reformulation of the concept might survive — but the prediction as stated (cosine-to-property counts, dominant single atom at ρ=1) fails on both falsification-relevant counts. Verdict fired via preregistered F1.
test.sanity: O1 pipeline oracle: a planted live SAE atom fed through the identical pipeline returns maxcos = 1.000 (>0.99 required). O2 relevance oracle: null distribution from 200 random unit directions gives 99.5th-pct max|cos| = 0.171; all 5 concept directions score 0.32–0.43, i.e. 2–2.5x above null — the measurement genuinely detects SAE alignment, so the flat split-count is a real result, not a broken meter. Run 1 additionally proved the gates work: they caught a real design flaw (shared topic component) before any verdict was issued.

stress.where: The word 'feature' itself: used simultaneously for (a) a direction in activation space, (b) an SAE dictionary latent, (c) a human-interpretable concept, (d) a causal mediator of behavior
stress.why: One term carries four operationalizations that empirically dissociate: SAE latents with high auto-interp scores often fail as causal mediators; probe directions that decode a concept often aren't the directions the model uses. Practitioners patch by qualifying ('functional feature', 'atomic feature', 'true feature') without a definition that predicts when the four coincide.

killed.name: Feature Role Signature (mandatory 4-axis certificate)
killed.why: Feature absorption (Chanin et al. 2024, documented in Gemma-Scope-class SAEs) breaks the ontology from inside: a latent labeled C="token starts with E" certifies as an 'aligned feature' — held-out probe AUC stays high (absorption only dents recall on a handful of tokens), precision@k of top activations is ~1.0 (every top-activating input is an E-word), interchange intervention along v moves first-letter behavior, and simulation scoring correlates well — yet the direction is gerrymandered and silently dead on "elephant" because a co-trained latent absorbed it. The certificate awards the reserved bare noun 'feature' to a non-atomic, absorbed direction, so the aligned region does not carve feature-hood at its joints (same failure for v=(v1+v2)/sqrt(2) of two real features with C="C1 or C2", which also clears all four axes). The certificate is also not even well-formed for arbitrary (v,L,C): med = interchange effect / full-layer patch effect divides by ~0 whenever C is not causally used at layer L for the chosen output metric (e.g., a sentiment latent, middle layer, syntax-continuation task), and int >= 0.8 simulation-Pearson is so rare in practice (Bills et al. 2023: top explained units average ~0.3-0.4) that the 'feature' region is nearly empty — a vocabulary that names almost nothing.

concept.name: Read/Write Feature Split (dual-basis typed features)
concept.definition: Ban the bare term 'feature direction'; every concept-C claim at layer L must name a typed pair (w, r) plus mismatch angle. Write-direction w: unit vector in the span of upstream-component outputs, fit by regressing component outputs against C labels. Read-direction r: argmax over unit vectors of the attribution-weighted alignment sum a_j * cos^2(r, row_j) where row_j ranges over input-weight rows of downstream components and a_j is that component's causal attribution to C-behavior (via path/attribution patching). Mismatch theta = arccos|<w,r>| is a mandatory reported quantity. Probe directions are a third derived object, not a feature.
concept.prediction: Across concepts and layers in one open model (e.g., Gemma-2-2B + Gemma-Scope), the mediation effect of patching along any candidate direction d is predicted by read-alignment cos^2(d, r) with R^2 >= 0.5, while held-out probe AUC of d adds < 0.05 incremental R^2; and projecting a decodable-but-non-mediating probe direction onto the read-cone yields a direction recovering >= 70% of the full-layer-patch behavioral effect. If read-alignment predicts mediation no better than probe accuracy does, the split is dead.
concept.reduces_to: The 'reading vs writing directions' remark in Elhage et al.'s Mathematical Framework, plus the amnesic-probing/LEACE result that decodable != used — possibly just formalizing folklore that already exists.

test.design: Falsification on gpt2-small (CPU; Gemma-2-2B out of budget — honest scope reduction, same logical structure), layers {5,8} resid_pre, last token, 6 word-category concepts, ONE intervention (projection patching along a unit direction d: move base activation's d-component to the source value). Per concept×layer group: read direction r = normalized gradient of the behavioral metric (category-vs-union logit contrast) at the hook — the steelman "what downstream reads"; candidate pool of 12 directions per group (probe diff-in-means p, r, mean-diff Δ-hat, p-orthogonalized-to-r d_nm, p/r and p/random mixes, 3 randoms) → 144 pooled points. Measured: normalized mediation m(d)=E(d)/E_full, read-alignment cos²(d,r), held-out probe AUC of d (different prompt templates). PREREGISTERED (committed 728571b BEFORE running, maevtica repo, experiments/read_write_split/PREREG.md): SUPPORTED iff R²[m~cos²] >= 0.50 AND incremental R² from adding AUC < 0.05 AND median E(r)/E_full recovery >= 0.70; REFUTED iff R²_read <= R²_AUC (concept's own kill clause) OR R²_read < 0.25 OR recovery < 0.35; else INCONCLUSIVE. One prereg amendment after run 1: the O2 code-correctness oracle had to use per-prompt Δ-hat (where patch=full-patch is a true identity), not the mean-diff direction; no decision threshold changed.
test.numbers: n=144 directions across 12 concept×layer groups. R²_read (m ~ cos²(d,r)) = 0.100 — far below the predicted >= 0.5. R²_AUC alone = 0.052; incremental R² of AUC over cos² = 0.020 (that sub-clause alone would have passed). Median read-direction recovery E(r)/E_full = 0.170 vs predicted >= 0.70 (per-group range 0.02–0.44). Decodable-but-non-mediating directions DO exist: d_nm qualified (AUC>=0.8, |m|<0.3) in 12/12 groups (mean AUC(d_nm)=0.98, mean m(d_nm)=0.019). Kill clause narrowly not triggered (0.100 > 0.052), but both hard REFUTED thresholds hit (R²_read < 0.25 AND recovery < 0.35). Diagnostic secondary: the bilinear predictor (d·Δ)(d·r) gets R² = 0.629 — mediation needs write-content alignment times read alignment; read-alignment alone is not the predictor the concept claims.
test.sanity: Oracles all passed on the final run: O1 effect exists — mean full-layer-patch effect 4.34 logits (per-group 2.1–9.5, all positive); O2 code correctness — patching along per-prompt Δ-hat must equal the full patch by construction: median |m−1| = 0.0000 (exact); O3 AUC pipeline — held-out AUC(probe) = 0.986, AUC(random) = 0.695 (inside preregistered [0.3,0.7]; inflated by the sign-free max(a,1−a) fold with small n — noted limit). The oracle discipline earned its keep again: run 1 returned BROKEN_MEASUREMENT because O2 was computed from the mean-diff direction (median |m−1| = 0.334) — a broken oracle implementation, caught and fixed without touching any decision threshold; primary numbers were identical across both runs.

concept.name: Behavioral Quotient Feature (intervention-equivalence class)
concept.definition: Fix layer L, input distribution D, and behavioral readout b (e.g., a task logit-diff). Two activation perturbations delta1, delta2 (norm <= rho) are equivalent iff E_D|b(x; h_L+delta1) - b(x; h_L+delta2)| < epsilon. A feature is a coordinate of the quotient space (activation space modulo behavior-preserving perturbations), estimated as top singular directions of the D-averaged Jacobian of b w.r.t. h_L. Feature claims are statements about quotient coordinates; SAE latents and probe directions get classified by which quotient coordinate(s) their vectors project onto. 'Interpretable concept' and 'causal mediator' coincide by construction iff they land on the same coordinate.
concept.prediction: Two computable clauses: (i) the effective rank k(L) (singular directions covering 90% of behavioral variance) stays within 10% when SAE dictionary width grows 4x, while latent count grows 4x — quotient dimension is a property of the model, not the dictionary; (ii) any two SAE latents whose decoder vectors project mainly onto the same quotient coordinate produce steering effects with logit-diff-vector cosine > 0.8 even when their decoder cosine is < 0.1. Falsified if same-coordinate latents steer incoherently or if effective rank tracks dictionary size.
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction / Distributed Alignment Search (Geiger et al.) — risk that this is DAS restated as a quotient construction, i.e., 'the aligned subspace' under a new name.

test.design: Preregistered in /home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/quotient_bq/PREREG.md BEFORE running (decision rule fixed there; code exp.py, output result.json + effects.npz). Setup: gpt2-small layer 6 resid_post, OpenAI v5 TopK SAEs at widths 32768 and 131072 (exactly 4x, same recipe/location — the only ready-made 4x pair on a CPU-sized model). Steer with alpha*unit(decoder_i) (alpha=0.5*mean resid norm=45.6) at all positions; effect vector = mean over 8 fixed probes of last-token logit diff; N=256 alive latents sampled per SAE, seed 0. Clause (i) rule: k90 (singular dirs covering 90% of centered effect variance) — supported if |Δ|/min ≤ 0.10, refuted if ratio ≥ 1.5, inconclusive if sample-limited (k90 ≥ 0.8N). Clause (ii) rule: quotient coords = top-k90 left singular vectors of ridge-fit map from 32k decoders to 32k effects (fit decoupled from 128k test set); qualifying 128k pairs = both dominance ≥0.5 (ladder to 0.4 if <5 pairs), same argmax coordinate, decoder |cos|<0.1; supported if median sign-corrected effect cos > 0.8 AND ≥0.2 above a fair null (random low-decoder-cos pairs), refuted if < 0.4. Overall: SUPPORTED iff both; REFUTED iff either refuted.
test.numbers: Clause (i): k90_32k=96, k90_128k=93 — 3.2% difference (bound 10%) while dictionary grew 4x → supported-i, and NOT sample-limited (96,93 << 0.8*256). Key diagnostic: decoder-geometry k90 = 148/143, i.e. behavioral rank (~95) sits clearly BELOW dictionary geometry rank — the rank is behavior-driven, not decoder-span-driven. Clause (ii): the premise population is EMPTY — 0 of 256 sampled 128k latents put ≥40% of quotient-projection energy on any single coordinate (K=96), so 0 qualifying pairs even after the preregistered 0.4 fallback → inconclusive_no_pairs (clause vacuous as operationalized). Fair null for pair cosines: 0.129. Exploratory (post-hoc, NOT decisive): over 500 low-decoder-cos 128k pairs, spearman(quotient-profile similarity, effect cosine) = 0.52 (graded quotient structure is real), but the 3 pairs with profile-sim > 0.8 had median effect cos 0.32 — far below the predicted 0.8, leaning AGAINST the strong coherence threshold.
test.sanity: All oracles passed, measurement valid: O1 split-half reliability (probes 0-3 vs 4-7) median cos = 0.828 across all 512 steered latents (threshold 0.6); O2 positive control (rescale 1.1*alpha, preregistered fallback since only 2 cross-SAE near-duplicate pairs existed) median effect cos = 0.999 (threshold 0.9); O3 mean effect norm 192-195 vs repeat-clean noise floor 0.0. No BROKEN_MEASUREMENT.

concept.name: Gated Feature (direction x context contract)
concept.definition: A feature is a pair (v, g): direction v plus a sparse gate g — a predicate of <= 5 literals over co-occurring SAE-latent activity ('latent j active / inactive') — such that the mediation effect of patching along v conditional on g(x)=1 exceeds threshold theta, with g fit by decision tree / lasso on conditional-patching outcomes and validated on held-out inputs (precision >= 0.7). The four senses of 'feature' are predicted to coincide inside the gate and dissociate outside it; an ungated direction is not a feature, it is a marginal average over contexts.
concept.prediction: For >= 25% of SAE latents whose unconditional mediation effect falls in the bottom quintile of their layer, there exists a learnable gate (<= 5 literals, held-out precision >= 0.7) under which their mediation effect exceeds the layer median by >= 3x — i.e., most 'causally dead but interpretable' latents are context-marginalization artifacts. Directly computable with conditional activation patching. Falsified if conditional patching rescues < 5% of dead latents.
concept.reduces_to: Circuits / feature-interaction analysis (input-conditional feature effects in attribution graphs) — possibly just renaming 'a feature only matters inside its circuit' with a fitting procedure attached.

survivors.name: Legibility coordinate (auto-interp fidelity as its own axis)

concept.name: Legibility coordinate (auto-interp fidelity as its own axis)
concept.definition: Legibility λ(atom) = simulate-and-score fidelity of the atom's natural-language label: correlation (or F1) between activations predicted from the label and true activations (Bills/EleutherAI auto-interp protocol). The ~38% auto-interp failure rate = fraction with λ below threshold. The representational move: treat λ as a coordinate ORTHOGONAL to read-fraction and coding sparsity — it isolates human-nameability from the causal and sparsity senses of 'feature'.
concept.prediction: Across atoms, λ is near-independent of read-fraction R and of coding sparsity κ: predict |Pearson r| < 0.2 for both. Corollaries: (i) among top-decile most-causal atoms a substantial share are illegible ('dark computation'); (ii) among the most legible atoms a substantial share are causally inert. Falsified if λ correlates strongly (|r|>0.5) with R or κ. NB: needs label simulation — if no cheap local path exists, designed_not_run with the design written down is the honest outcome.
concept.reduces_to: Auto-interp / explanation scoring (Bills et al.). Only new content is the asserted orthogonality to the causal and sparsity axes.

test.design: Cheap local label-simulation path DID exist, so this ran instead of designed_not_run: λ = auto-interp fidelity via a unigram-label simulator (Bills-style simulation with a lexical label instead of an LLM labeler) — label = top-10 tokens by train-half mean activation, simulate held-out activations from the label, λ = Pearson(sim, actual). Setup: gpt2-small + jbloom SAE at blocks.8.hook_resid_pre; corpus = wikitext-103 test (local parquet), 64 seqs x 128 tokens split even/odd into train/test halves; n=200 randomly sampled alive atoms (fire >=20x in each half, rate <0.5, seed 0). Per atom: λ; κ axis = log10 firing rate; R = read-fraction (u'Gu)/λmax(G) with downstream reader Gram over layers >=8 (reused from read_fraction/exp.py); C (secondary) = mean Δloss from per-atom ablation on 8 held-out seqs. Decision rule FROZEN and committed BEFORE running (maevtica commit 9d8049c, research_cycle/experiments/legibility_coordinate/PREREG.md): SUPPORTED iff |Pearson(λ,R)|<0.20 AND |Pearson(λ,log10 f)|<0.20; REFUTED iff either >0.50; else INCONCLUSIVE; corollaries (dark-computation / inert-legible shares) secondary and non-gating, gated by their own oracle O4. Code: experiments/legibility_coordinate/exp.py (commit eeaeef0).
test.numbers: n=200 atoms. PRIMARY: Pearson(λ,R) = -0.084 (p=0.24), Pearson(λ,log10 f) = -0.093 (p=0.19) — both well inside the preregistered |r|<0.2 support band, nowhere near the |r|>0.5 falsification bar. Robustness (all consistent): Spearman(λ,R) -0.058, Spearman(λ,log f) -0.089, Pearson(λ,raw f) -0.102, K=50 label: r_R -0.076, r_κ -0.041. λ has real spread (mean 0.155, range -0.008..0.999, std 0.198) — the axis is not degenerate. Caveat: R range across SAE decoder atoms is narrow (0.028-0.105; restriction of range works against detecting r_R), κ spans 0.005-0.111 firing rate. Corollary shares (dark computation / legible-but-inert) pending: the 200-atom ablation loop (~20 min CPU) was still running at report time; it appends to RESULT.txt and is secondary/non-gating per prereg. Honest scope: single small model, single SAE/layer, unigram simulator is a LOWER BOUND on LLM auto-interp legibility, one seed — survived, not proven.
test.sanity: All three gating oracles passed on the full run: O1 positive control — a synthetic atom firing exactly on the corpus's most frequent token measured λ = 1.000 (rule: >0.9), and a synthetic noise atom measured λ = -0.008 (rule: <0.15), so the simulator detects legibility when present and reports none when absent; O2 — std(λ)=0.198 > 0.03, real atoms span 0.00-0.999, axis not degenerate; O3 (guards the 2026-07-03 .norm-bug class) — reader-Gram eigvec rho_top/rho_bot ratio >> 5 and rho_rand 6.940 vs analytic expectation 6.948. A pipeline smoke test (n=8) ran first and behaved sanely. BROKEN_MEASUREMENT branch existed in code and did not trigger.

concept.name: Behavioral Quotient Feature (intervention-equivalence class)
concept.definition: Fix layer L, input distribution D, and behavioral readout b (e.g., a task logit-diff). Two activation perturbations delta1, delta2 (norm <= rho) are equivalent iff E_D|b(x; h_L+delta1) - b(x; h_L+delta2)| < epsilon. A feature is a coordinate of the quotient space (activation space modulo behavior-preserving perturbations), estimated as top singular directions of the D-averaged Jacobian of b w.r.t. h_L. Feature claims are statements about quotient coordinates; SAE latents and probe directions get classified by which quotient coordinate(s) their vectors project onto. 'Interpretable concept' and 'causal mediator' coincide by construction iff they land on the same coordinate.
concept.prediction: Two computable clauses: (i) the effective rank k(L) (singular directions covering 90% of behavioral variance) stays within 10% when SAE dictionary width grows 4x, while latent count grows 4x — quotient dimension is a property of the model, not the dictionary; (ii) any two SAE latents whose decoder vectors project mainly onto the same quotient coordinate produce steering effects with logit-diff-vector cosine > 0.8 even when their decoder cosine is < 0.1. Falsified if same-coordinate latents steer incoherently or if effective rank tracks dictionary size.

Clause (ii) rule: quotient coords = top-k90 left singular vectors of ridge-fit map from 32k decoders to 32k effects (fit decoupled from 128k test set); qualifying 128k pairs = both dominance ≥0.5 (ladder to 0.4 if <5 pairs), same argmax coordinate, decoder |cos|<0.1; supported if median sign-corrected effect cos > 0.8 AND ≥0.2 above a fair null (random low-decoder-cos pairs), refuted if < 0.4. Key diagnostic: decoder-geometry k90 = 148/143, i.e. behavioral rank (~95) sits clearly BELOW dictionary geometry rank — the rank is behavior-driven, not decoder-span-driven. Clause (ii): the premise population is EMPTY — 0 of 256 sampled 128k latents put ≥40% of quotient-projection energy on any single coordinate (K=96), so 0 qualifying pairs even after the preregistered 0.4 fallback → inconclusive_no_pairs (clause vacuous as operationalized). Exploratory (post-hoc, NOT decisive): over 500 low-decoder-cos 128k pairs, spearman(quotient-profile similarity, effect cosine) = 0.52 (graded quotient structure is real), but the 3 pairs with profile-sim > 0.8 had median effect cos 0.32 — far below the predicted 0.8, leaning AGAINST the strong coherence threshold.
test.sanity: All oracles passed, measurement valid: O1 split-half reliability (probes 0-3 vs 4-7) median cos = 0.828 across all 512 steered latents (threshold 0.6); O2 positive control (rescale 1.1*alpha, preregistered fallback since only 2 cross-SAE near-duplicate pairs existed) median effect cos = 0.999 (threshold 0.9); O3 mean effect norm 192-195 vs repeat-clean noise floor 0.0. No BROKEN_MEASUREMENT.

concept.name: Gated Feature (direction x context contract)
concept.definition: A feature is a pair (v, g): direction v plus a sparse gate g — a predicate of <= 5 literals over co-occurring SAE-latent activity ('latent j active / inactive') — such that the mediation effect of patching along v conditional on g(x)=1 exceeds threshold theta, with g fit by decision tree / lasso on conditional-patching outcomes and validated on held-out inputs (precision >= 0.7). The four senses of 'feature' are predicted to coincide inside the gate and dissociate outside it; an ungated direction is not a feature, it is a marginal average over contexts.
concept.prediction: For >= 25% of SAE latents whose unconditional mediation effect falls in the bottom quintile of their layer, there exists a learnable gate (<= 5 literals, held-out precision >= 0.7) under which their mediation effect exceeds the layer median by >= 3x — i.e., most 'causally dead but interpretable' latents are context-marginalization artifacts. Directly computable with conditional activation patching. Falsified if conditional patching rescues < 5% of dead latents.
concept.reduces_to: Circuits / feature-interaction analysis (input-conditional feature effects in attribution graphs) — possibly just renaming 'a feature only matters inside its circuit' with a fitting procedure attached.

controls.notes:
Calibration run of the campaign's adversary+historian bar (cycle.workflow.js: schemas L58-64, prompts L78-79, gate L136-142), domain "mechanistic interpretability". 2 known-real + 2 distractor concepts.

KNOWN-GOOD (both PASSED, as expected):
- Superposition (N>d features in non-orthogonal dirs; predicts sparsity-driven geometric phase structure + SAEs recover >neurons monosemantic feats; prior art: sparse coding/compressed sensing). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=T. Gate: separable -> survives. ✓
- Induction head (prefix-match 2-head copy; predicts ablation kills ICL + emergence=training phase change; prior art: n-gram/copy). adv: survives=T, is_rename=F. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=T. Gate: separable -> survives. ✓

DISTRACTORS (both KILLED, as expected):
- "Neural resonance" (activations 'harmonize' -> understanding; circular, no failure boundary; = cross-layer activation correlation/CKA in a metaphor). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=F. Gate -> killed. ✓
- "Semantic embedding direction" (a direction whose addition changes concept output = CAV/TCAV/steering vector renamed; prediction identical to prior art). adv: survives=F, is_rename=T. hist: is_rediscovery=T, separable_prediction=F. Gate -> killed. ✓

VERDICT: 2/2 good passed, 2/2 distractors killed -> judges discriminate; campaign verdicts trustworthy on this sample.

CAVEAT (the real risk to watch): both good concepts survive via the historian's separable_prediction escape hatch, NOT the strict clearlyNovel path (both are name-level rediscoveries) — this is the intended CRC-style mechanism, and it is the single load-bearing flag. Trustworthiness hinges entirely on the historian withholding separable_prediction from renames whose prediction matches prior art (it did here) while granting it to sharpened concepts with a divergent, experimentally-separable prediction (it did here). A too-generous historian marking a rename's prediction 'separable' is the one failure mode that would let a distractor through; recommend keeping controlEvery low and treating any separable_prediction=T on a concept the adversary flags is_rename=T as suspect. Files: research_cycle/cycle.workflow.js, campaigns/night-2026-07-04/round1.json.

report:
# Отчёт разведки: заглянули внутрь ИИ-модели

**Кампания: 3 идеи проверены · 2 опровергнуты · 1 выжила**

---

## 1. Куда светили фонариком и зачем

Внутри ИИ-модели миллионы «шестерёнок», и никто толком не знает, какая за что отвечает. Есть популярный инструмент — назовём его **словарь**: он пытается разложить внутреннюю кашу модели на отдельные понятные кусочки («это про собак», «это про запятые»).

Проблема, с которой мы пришли: само слово «понятие» в этой науке означает четыре разные вещи одновременно — направление в математике, кусочек словаря, человеческую идею и рычаг поведения. Все их путают. Мы попробовали построить три отдельные «линейки», которые эти смыслы разлепляют. Каждую линейку проверили честным экспериментом: **правило «зачёт/незачёт» записывали и фиксировали ДО запуска**, чтобы нельзя было подогнать ответ.

## 2. Интересные мысли, найденные по дороге

- **Словарь мутноват для всего.** Ни одно из проверенных понятий словарь не поймал чисто — лучший результат далёк от идеала. Не «одни понятия ловятся, другие нет», а «всё ловится наполовину». Это неожиданно и само по себе находка.
- **Похоже, понятия живут не на отдельных полочках, а на целых стеллажах.** Когда мы мерили не «один кусочек словаря», а «группу кусочков вместе», картинка вдруг начинала сходиться. Это главная зацепка на будущее.
- **Приборы у нас работали.** В каждый опыт мы подкладывали эталон («вот заведомо правильный ответ, прибор обязан показать 100%») — и прибор показывал ровно 100%, а случайный шум показывал почти ноль. Значит наши опровержения настоящие, а не сломанный градусник.

## 3. Что построили — и честно сломали

Большинство лотков при промывке золота пустые. Говорим прямо: два из трёх — пустые.

### ✗ Идея «редкость» — опровергнута
**Догадка:** чем реже понятие «загорается» в тексте, тем легче словарю его найти. Хотели предсказывать заранее, что словарь поймает, а что упустит.
**Что показал опыт:** почти ничего не предсказывает. Мы обещали конкретные цифры («редкие ловятся вот настолько, частые — вот настолько») — цифры провалились в 80% случаев. Самое редкое понятие ловилось *хуже*, чем одно из самых частых. Перепроверили на другом «этаже» модели — та же картина. Слабый намёк на связь остался, но заявленное правило — мёртвое.

### ✗ Идея «толщина» — опровергнута
**Догадка:** «толстые» понятия (которым нужно несколько полочек) словарь дробит на много кусочков, а «тонкие» ловит одним кусочком.
**Что показал опыт:** количество кусочков вообще не растёт с толщиной — у тонких в среднем 2.6 кусочка, у толстых 2.5. И ни одно тонкое понятие не поймалось одним чистым кусочком, как обещала догадка. Отдельная честность: первый прогон мы сами забраковали до вердикта — нашли изъян в постановке, починили по заранее записанным правилам, и только второй, чистый прогон дал вердикт.

### ✓ Идея «называемость» — выжила
**Догадка:** то, можно ли кусочку словаря дать честное человеческое имя, — это **отдельное свойство**. Оно не связано ни с тем, насколько кусочек важен для работы модели, ни с тем, как часто он загорается.
**Что показал опыт:** проверили 200 кусочков — связи действительно почти нет, ровно как предсказывали. То есть внутри модели могут быть важные, но безымянные шестерёнки — и наоборот, красиво названные, но бесполезные.
**Оговорки, без которых нечестно:** «выжила» — не «доказана». Проверено на одной небольшой модели, одним способом придумывания имён, и одна из линеек в этом опыте имела узкий разброс (это облегчает прохождение теста). Нужна перепроверка на другом материале.

## 4. Что стоит искать следующей экспедиции

1. **Стеллажи вместо полочек.** Переделать идею «толщины» под группы кусочков — единственное место, где цифры росли как надо.
2. **Тёмные шестерёнки.** Раз важность и называемость независимы — прицельно найти важные-но-безымянные части модели. Это самое тревожное и самое ценное следствие выжившей идеи.
3. **Почему всё мутное.** Выяснить, почему ни одно понятие не ловится словарём чисто — это может быть свойство самого инструмента.
4. **Перепроверка выжившей** на модели побольше и с другим способом называния.

---

<details>
<summary>Детали по запросу (техническое)</summary>

**Домен:** mechanistic interpretability; терминологический стресс — слово *feature* употребляется в 4 смыслах (направление в activation space / SAE-латент / человеческий концепт / каузальный медиатор).

**Общий сетап:** gpt2-small (CPU), pretrained SAE jbloom/GPT2-Small-SAEs (24 576 атомов), resid_pre на слоях 6/7/8. Все три эксперимента преререгистрированы (decision rules зафиксированы до запуска, файлы PREREG.md закоммичены).

**1. Coding sparsity κ(P) — REFUTED.** κ = −log2(firing rate); recovery = max cosine между probe-направлением d_P и decoder-атомами. n=10 свойств, слой 8: Spearman ρ=0.418, p=0.117 (порог поддержки был ρ≥0.6, p<0.05); конкретный median-split (<0.5 / >0.8) нарушен в 80% случаев — ни одно свойство не превысило maxcos 0.72; pronouns (κ=7.37) → 0.55 против the (κ=4.67) → 0.72. Реплика на слое 6: ρ=0.455, нарушения те же. Оракулы: подложенный decoder-атом → maxcos=1.0000; random baseline 0.135; все d_P с held-out ROC-AUC 0.91–0.99.

**2. Carrier rank ρ(P) — REFUTED (по пререгистрированному критерию F1).** Ground-truth ρ через объединения 5 базовых концептов (ρ=1/2/3). Run 1 сам завалил validity-гейты (общий «topic vs neutral» компонент) → INCONCLUSIVE_DESIGN; амендмент (центрирование diff-ов) пререгистрирован до run 2. Run 2: spearman(ρ, count@0.3)=0.063 (нужно >0.3); средний счёт кусочков 2.6 (ρ=1) vs 2.5 (ρ=3); ни один ρ=1 концепт не достиг maxcos>0.8 (максимум 0.43). Зато subspace-проекционная метрика росла: 2.6 → 15.3 → 19 — зацепка №1 из раздела 4. Оракулы: planted atom → 1.000; null 99.5-й перцентиль 0.171 против наблюдаемых 0.32–0.43.

**3. Legibility λ — SUPPORTED.** λ = fidelity Bills-style симуляции по униграммному лейблу (top-10 токенов, train/test split), n=200 живых атомов, wikitext-103. Pearson(λ, read-fraction R) = −0.084, Pearson(λ, log10 f) = −0.093 — оба внутри пререгистрированной полосы |r|<0.2 (порог опровержения |r|>0.5). Робастность: Spearman и K=50-лейбл согласны; λ не вырождена (std 0.198, диапазон до 0.999). Главный кавеат: узкий диапазон R по декодер-атомам (0.028–0.105) — restriction of range снижает мощность детекции корреляции; плюс одна модель, один слой, лексический симулятор вместо LLM-лейблера.

**Файлы:** scratchpad/{prereg_kappa_sae.md, kappa_sae.py, result.json, result_L6.json}; maevtica research_cycle/experiments/legibility_coordinate/{PREREG.md (commit 9d8049c), exp.py (commit eeaeef0)}.

**Бэклог кампании исчерпан** (все 3 концепта из очереди протестированы).

</details>
